title: Garlic-infused, Velvety Russian Leek Borscht
description: Umami-rich, velvety, and comforting. A classic Russian dish made with tender leeks, fragrant garlic, and a hint of fermentation.

ingredients.name: Leeks
ingredients.notes: white and light green parts only

instructions: In a large copper pot, heat the sunflower oil over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and sauté for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the sliced leeks and cook for 5 minutes, until they start to soften.
Add the fermented Korean chili paste, beetroot juice, vegetable broth, salt, and black pepper.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Use an immersion blender to puree the soup until smooth.
Stir in the creme fraiche and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until heated through.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the borscht hot, garnished with chopped fresh dill or chives if desired.
Refrigerate or freeze the remaining soup for future meals.
Reheat the borscht gently before serving.
